div.head: The Minister in Haiti (Armour) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 47. Reference Legation’s telegram No. 39, April 7, 9 p.m.; despatch No. 11 of April 7.Despatch No. 11 not printed. The Hudicourt resolution referred to therein was adopted yesterday by a [vote] of 18 [12?] to 7 in the Senate after a heated discussion which lasted several hours. The debate in support of the resolution was led by Senator Pradel in the course of which he severely criticised the American administration in Haiti. A number of the Senators took the position that they did not favor an immediate vote but swung over to vote with the majority. It is not known whether the Government attempted to exercise any pressure to prevent passage of this resolution.
